prompt:
You are a PROOF-FILLER. Replace the `sorry` placeholders for the listed declarations with complete, type-correct Lean proofs. Do NOT change statement signatures; do NOT weaken hypotheses.

## Integrity (HARD RULES — non-negotiable)
- Close a `sorry` ONLY with a genuine proof. NEVER discharge it with a device that makes the file compile without actually proving the statement: no new `axiom`, no `admit`, no unproven auxiliary `axiom`/lemma cited by `exact`, no `native_decide`/`decide` on a claim that is not genuinely decidable, no `@[implemented_by]`/`@[extern]` or `sorry` hidden inside a helper. Introducing an axiom for the hard part is a FAILURE, not a pass — it silently injects an assumption into the library.
- If a declaration is genuinely beyond you this turn, LEAVE its `sorry` exactly as it is (do not replace it with a fake discharge) and report honestly that you could not prove it, naming the decl and the concrete blocker (missing lemma, unclear step, integrability side-condition, etc.). A `sorry` left in place and reported is the CORRECT outcome when you don't know the proof; a fake discharge is never acceptable.

Lean files live under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling
Target declarations: coupling_integrable_mul, integral_signedTail_fst, integral_signedTail_snd, fiber_integral_pi, integrable_tail_fst_prod, integrable_tail_snd_prod, integrable_bigPhi, integral_prod_eq_integral_fiber, mean_fst_tail, mean_snd_tail, integrable_survFst_sub, integrable_survSnd_sub

## Instructions
File: CausalSmith/Substrate/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/HoeffdingFubini.lean (Lean 4 + Mathlib, repo root /<repo-root>/CausalSmith).

Fill the 12 `sorry`s. EDIT ONLY THIS FILE. Do not weaken/change any statement or hypothesis, no axioms, no new `sorry`. You MAY use the sorried lemmas of `TailIntegral.lean` and `Survival.lean` as given (they are being proven in parallel). Verify: `lake build CausalSmith.Substrate.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.HoeffdingFubini` (expect only `sorry` warnings from the other two files).

Read `TailIntegral.lean` and `Survival.lean` first for the API. Key facts available:
- `tailInd a s = if s < a then 1 else 0` (simp lemma `tailInd_apply`); `signedTail a s = tailInd a s - tailInd 0 s`.
- `integral_signedTail (a) : ∫ s, signedTail a s ∂volume = a` (PROVEN).
- `integral_signedTail_prod (x y) : ∫ q : ℝ×ℝ, signedTail x q.1 * signedTail y q.2 ∂(volume.prod volume) = x*y` (PROVEN).
- `integral_norm_signedTail_prod (x y) : ∫ q, ‖signedTail x q.1 * signedTail y q.2‖ ∂(volume.prod volume) = |x|*|y|`.
- `integral_abs_signedTail (a) : ∫ s, |signedTail a s| = |a|`; `measurable_signedTail_uncurry`; `abs_signedTail_le_one`; `integrable_signedTail`.
- `survFst π s = (π (Prod.fst ⁻¹' Ioi s)).toReal`, `survSnd`, `jointSurv π s t = (π (Ioi s ×ˢ Ioi t)).toReal`.
- `coupling_fst_memLp h hμ : MemLp (fun p : ℝ×ℝ => p.1) 2 π`, `coupling_snd_memLp` (PROVEN in this file), `coupling_integral_fst/snd` (PROVEN).

Always open with `letI : IsProbabilityMeasure π := h.isProbabilityMeasure`.

Guidance:
- `coupling_integrable_mul`: `MemLp.integrable_mul (coupling_fst_memLp h hμ) (coupling_snd_memLp h hν)` (2 and 2 form a `HolderTriple` to 1); massage `(f*g)` vs `fun p => p.1*p.2` with `Pi.mul_apply`/`simpa`.
- `integral_signedTail_fst h s : ∫ p, signedTail p.1 s ∂π = survFst π s - tailInd 0 s`. Note `signedTail p.1 s = (Prod.fst ⁻¹' Ioi s).indicator (fun _ => (1:ℝ)) p - tailInd 0 s` pointwise in `p` (check with `tailInd_apply`, `Set.indicator_apply`, `Set.mem_Ioi`). Then `integral_sub` (indicator is integrable: bounded, `π` finite), `integral_indicator_one` (measurable set: `measurableSet_Ioi.preimage measurable_fst`), `integral_const`, `measure_univ`.
- `fiber_integral_pi`: expand `signedTail p.1 s * signedTail p.2 t = (tailInd p.1 s)(tailInd p.2 t) - tailInd 0 t * tailInd p.1 s - tailInd 0 s * tailInd p.2 t + tailInd 0 s * tailInd 0 t` (`ring_nf` after `simp only [signedTail]`; note `tailInd 0 s`, `tailInd 0 t` are CONSTANTS in `p`). Integrate the four terms: `tailInd p.1 s * tailInd p.2 t` is the indicator of `Ioi s ×ˢ Ioi t` at `p` (prove by `funext`/`simp [tailInd_apply, Set.indicator_apply, Set.mem_prod]`, `split_ifs <;> simp`), giving `jointSurv`. Use `integral_add`/`integral_sub` with each piece integrable (all are indicators of measurable sets under a finite measure).
- `integrable_tail_fst_prod : Integrable (fun z : ℝ × (ℝ×ℝ) => signedTail z.2.1 z.1) (volume.prod π)`. Use `MeasureTheory.integrable_prod_iff'` (swap-orientation variant — pick whichever of `integrable_prod_iff`/`integrable_prod_iff'` matches; here the *first* factor is `volume`, so you want the criterion `(∀ᵐ p ∂π, Integrable (fun s => f (s,p)) volume) ∧ Integrable (fun p => ∫ s, ‖f (s,p)‖ ∂volume) π` — that is `integrable_prod_iff'`). Inner integrability = `integrable_signedTail p.1`; inner norm integral = `|p.1|` by `integral_abs_signedTail`; `fun p => |p.1|` is `π`-integrable since `MemLp _ 2 π` on a finite measure gives `Integrable` (`MemLp.integrable (by norm_num)`), then `.abs`. AEStronglyMeasurable from `measurable_signedTail_uncurry` composed with `fun z => (z.2.1, z.1)` (measurable), `.aestronglyMeasurable`.
- `integrable_bigPhi : Integrable (fun z : (ℝ×ℝ)×(ℝ×ℝ) => signedTail z.2.1 z.1.1 * signedTail z.2.2 z.1.2) ((volume.prod volume).prod π)`. Same pattern with `integrable_prod_iff'`: for a.e. `p`, `fun q => signedTail p.1 q.1 * signedTail p.2 q.2` is integrable on `volume.prod volume` (`integrable_signedTail_prod`); and `fun p => ∫ q, ‖...‖ ∂(volume.prod volume) = fun p => |p.1| * |p.2|` by `integral_norm_signedTail_prod`, which is `π`-integrable = `(coupling_integrable_mul h hμ hν).abs` up to `abs_mul`. Joint measurability: build from `measurable_signedTail_uncurry` and the measurable maps `z ↦ (z.2.1, z.1.1)`, `z ↦ (z.2.2, z.1.2)`, then `.mul`.
- `integral_prod_eq_integral_fiber`: `∫ p, p.1*p.2 ∂π = ∫ p, (∫ q, signedTail p.1 q.1 * signedTail p.2 q.2 ∂(volume.prod volume)) ∂π` by `integral_congr_ae`/`simp only [integral_signedTail_prod]`, then apply `MeasureTheory.integral_integral_swap` to `f q p := signedTail p.1 q.1 * signedTail p.2 q.2` with `Function.uncurry f = ` the function of `integrable_bigPhi` (check `Function.uncurry` unfolds definitionally; `simpa [Function.uncurry]`). Note the swap statement gives `∫ q, ∫ p, f q p ∂π ∂(vol.prod vol) = ∫ p, ∫ q, f q p ∂(vol.prod vol) ∂π`; orient accordingly. SFinite instances come from `IsProbabilityMeasure π`.
- `mean_fst_tail : ∫ x, x ∂μ = ∫ s, (survFst π s - tailInd 0 s) ∂volume`. Rewrite LHS via `(coupling_integral_fst h hμ).symm` to `∫ p, p.1 ∂π`; then `p.1 = ∫ s, signedTail p.1 s` (`integral_signedTail`), `integral_integral_swap` on `integrable_tail_fst_prod` (orientation `volume.prod π`, so `Integrable.integral_prod_left`-compatible), then `integral_signedTail_fst h`.
- `integrable_survFst_sub`: `(integrable_tail_fst_prod h hμ).integral_prod_left` gives `Integrable (fun s => ∫ p, signedTail p.1 s ∂π) volume`; rewrite the integrand with `integral_signedTail_fst h` via `Integrable.congr`.
- `_snd` variants mirror exactly.

Do not restate or rename anything; `Hoeffding.lean` and `Optimality.lean` depend on these exact signatures.

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you genuinely closed, which you left as `sorry` (with the concrete blocker for each), and — required — confirm you introduced NO `axiom`/`admit`/other non-proof discharge. No JSON required.
